title: German Breaded Schnitzel
description: A crispy breaded schnitzel dish, typically served with a side of fries. This recipe is a German-inspired twist on the classic dish.
instructions: Season the pork cutlets with salt and pepper.
Dredge the pork cutlets in flour, shaking off any excess.
Dip the floured cutlets in the beaten eggs, making sure they're fully coated.
Roll the egg-coated cutlets in the breaded crumbs, pressing the crumbs onto the meat to ensure they stick.
Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the breaded cutlets to the pan and fry for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
Transfer the cutlets to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
Reduce the heat to medium and fry the streaky bacon until crispy.
Serve the schnitzel hot with a side of fries and a dollop of rich mustard.
